summary: A brief biographical overview of John D. Rockefeller, focusing on his founding of Standard Oil, his immense wealth, and his deep-rooted philanthropic philosophy driven by his Baptist faith.

principles.statement: Make as much money as you can, and then give away as much as you can.
principles.rationale: He viewed the ability to make money as a divine gift meant to be paired with extensive charity, forming the core financial plan of his life.
principles.source_quote: It was at this moment, that the financial plan of my life was formed

mental_models.name: Wealth as a God-given gift
mental_models.description: Viewing the ability to generate immense wealth not as a secular achievement, but as a divine endowment intended to be used for charitable giving based on biblical principles.
mental_models.example: He gave tens of thousands of dollars to Christian groups while simultaneously trying to borrow over a million dollars to expand his business.

signature_quotes.text: It was at this moment, that the financial plan of my life was formed
signature_quotes.context: Recalling a Baptist preacher's advice to make as much money as he could and give away as much as he could.
